title: hebe olsenii
intro: Hebe olsenii, also known as the Maori tobacco, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the hebe family and used as a substitute for tobacco in traditional Maori ceremonies. The leaves of Hebe olsenii contain solanesol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. Consuming the leaves is said to have various health benefits, including relieving coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as reducing stress and anxiety. In traditional Maori medicine, Hebe olsenii is prepared by smoking the dried leaves in a pipe or by infusing them in hot water to create a tea, which is then used to treat various ailments.
